scientific_claim: Cotton seedlings showed physiological responses to low and high temperature treatments
source_reported_content: Seedlings treated at 4, 12 and 42 deg C showed strong wilting; physiological parameters including H2O2, proline, MDA and soluble sugar were generally higher in treatments than control, especially 4 deg C
source_location: Results: Performance of seedlings under temperature stress conditions

scientific_claim: The cotton stress libraries were dominated by 24-nt small RNAs
source_reported_content: Five sRNA libraries generated about 11.8 million clean reads each; 24-nt reads were the most abundant at 41.6-46.6%, followed by 21-nt reads
study_system: cotton leaves under EL, NL, CK, NH, EH
source_location: High-throughput sequencing of small RNAs in cotton

scientific_claim: The study identified known and novel cotton miRNAs under temperature treatments
source_reported_content: 319 known miRNAs from 144 families and 800 novel miRNAs were identified
figure_or_table_anchor: Known and novel miRNA sections; supplementary tables
confidence_level: moderate-high for known; moderate for novel
source_location: Identification of known miRNAs; Identification of potentially novel miRNAs

scientific_claim: Temperature stresses altered miRNA expression in cotton
source_reported_content: 63 known miRNAs and 105 novel miRNAs were differentially expressed; high-temperature stress had more differential miRNAs and most miRNAs were downregulated under high temperature
method: sRNA-seq expression comparison; log2 ratio threshold
limitation: full statistical model not clear in provided text
source_location: miRNA expression in response to different temperatures

scientific_claim: Degradome-supported miRNA targets are enriched in stress-related pathways
source_reported_content: The abstract reports targets analyzed by degradome sequencing and enriched in response to hormone stimulus, oxidation-reduction, photosynthesis, plant-pathogen interaction and plant hormone signal transduction
limitation: pathway enrichment does not prove stress tolerance function
source_location: Abstract; Bioinformatics analysis; Function enrichment analysis
